message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:

**Task 1 — anthropicprinciple.ai doc reorganization:**
Merge `docs/architecture/ARCHITECTURE.md` into `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`, move `docs/architecture/SYSTEM.md` to `docs/SYSTEM.md`, delete the originals, and update all active references across the codebase.

**Task 2 — white-hat-label initial scaffold:**
Reconstruct the entire architecture folder system and plan folder structure as a white-label starter project suitable for sharing on GitHub. Referenced `docs/init/setup.txt` for requirements. Create `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`, `docs/SYSTEM.md`, full `docs/architecture/` folder with templates, `context/summaries/` for session management, `CLAUDE.md`, and `README.md`. Remove all anthropicprinciple references.

**Task 3 — white-hat-label new files + CLAUDE.md improvement:**
Three new architecture files and a `template-examples/` folder had been manually added (CHECKPOINTS.md, FE-VISUALISATION.md, six-hats.md, and template-examples/ with GEMINI-CONSULTANCY.md, SERVERSIDE.md, STANDARDS.md). Review and genericize all, remove anthropicprinciple references. Improve CLAUDE.md as a cleaner white-label skeleton with TBA markers where appropriate.

**Task 4 — white-hat-label plan system:**
Set up a `docs/plan/` folder based on the anthropicprinciple plan folder structure. Create: a `tasklist.md` template, a `handoff_template.md`, an `example-tasklist.md` showing tasks to set up the starter project, an `example-handoff_date.md` for the same session, and a copy of `plan-rules.md`.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
- White-label boilerplate starter project for AI-assisted (Claude Code) web development
- Conventional Commits git workflow (`type(scope): description`)
- CSS custom properties as single source of truth (`styles/theme.css`)
- Mobile-first responsive design (`min-width` only breakpoints)
- G1–G13 global constraints (do-not-break patterns)
- Architecture Decision Records (ADRs) in DECISIONS.md
- Session handoff system (plan-rules.md, tasklist.md, handoff_YYYY-MM-DD.md)
- Six Hats thinking model for AI sessions
- Gemini MCP "second model" consultancy protocol
- Auto-checkpoint system for mid-session state preservation
- FE visualisation debugging approach (Python snap default, Playwright for viewport control)
- 12-column grid, 1rem gutter, 288px min, 1440px max
- WCAG 2.1 AA compliance
- CSS load order: `theme.css` → `global.css` → `grid.css` → `components.css` → `custom.css` → `utilities.css`
- JS IIFE module pattern, no globals, `const`/`let` only
- `localStorage` sanitisation before use
- `requestAnimationFrame` + `visibilitychange` for animation
- Schema.org JSON-LD, Open Graph meta tags
- `logger.js` — separate dev logger, buffered in memory

3. Files and Code Sections:

**anthropicprinciple project:**

- `/Users/mat/Claudette/Cowork/projects/anthropicprinciple/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`
  - Merged result of both ARCHITECTURE.md files. Contains: What It Is, Platform & Deployment, Core Structural Decisions (8), File Structure, Protected Files, Page/Script Dependency Diagram, CSS Architecture (clock + non-clock), Clock Engine (grid, 30s cycle, 4 patterns, performance), Data Flow, Countdown Zero State, Favicon Animator, Control Panel, Logger, Conventions Quick Reference, What We Never Do table. Committed `5b1d135`.

- `/Users/mat/Claudette/Cowork/projects/anthropicprinciple/docs/SYSTEM.md`
  - Moved from `docs/architecture/SYSTEM.md`. Developer rules: naming conventions (CSS/JS/files), file organisation map, CSS rules, JS rules, HTML rules, commit conventions.

- References updated in: `README.md`, `docs/architecture/CLAUDE_ARCHITECTURE.md`, `docs/architecture/REFLECTIVE-SYNC.md`, `docs/architecture/six-hats.md`, `docs/architecture/CODEBASE-AUDIT.md`, `docs/plan/plan-rules.md`

**white-hat-label project:**

- `/CLAUDE.md` — Comprehensive project instructions. Key sections: Summary (TBA placeholder), Stack table with optional stack additions listed, Repository & Deployment, File Structure (full tree including docs/architecture/ subfolder listing all 11 architecture docs + template-examples/), Technical Development Approach (HTML/CSS/JS subsections), UX Goals, SEO Features, Performance Features, Content Summary (TBA), Layout & Style Guide (12-col grid, typography table, colour palette with LM/DM tokens, spacing scale, breakpoints), CSS Conventions, Standards Compliance, Skills table (frontend-standards, git-commit-messaging, fe-visualisation), Test Programme (both checklists), Common Commands, Git Workflow (references context/summaries/plan-rules.md), Logging System.

- `/README.md` — Public-facing: project description, live site link, stack list, local dev command, repo structure, documentation links.

- `/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` — Generic architecture template with [PROJECT_NAME] placeholders. Sections: What It Is, Platform & Deployment, Core Structural Decisions (6 generic ones: zero deps, CSS custom properties, mobile-first, CSS-first, no inline styles, localStorage sanitisation), File Structure, Protected Files (TBA), Page/Script Dependency Diagram, CSS Architecture, Data Flow diagram, Conventions Quick Reference, What We Never Do table.

- `/docs/SYSTEM.md` — Developer rules: naming conventions (CSS/JS/files tables), File Organisation map, CSS Rules, JS Rules, HTML Rules, Commit Conventions table.

- `/docs/architecture/CORE_PATTERNS.md` — G1–G13 generic constraints table, Performance Floor table, "Why" history for key constraints (G1, G2, G3/G4, G5, G8, G12), 7 detailed code patterns with correct/wrong examples (CSS custom properties, mobile-first breakpoints, rAF throttle, visibilitychange, localStorage sanitisation, safe external links, safe DOM content), Quick Regression Checklist.

- `/docs/architecture/DECISIONS.md` — ADR template with ADR-001 (placeholder), ADR-002 (CSS custom properties), ADR-003 (mobile-first min-width).

- `/docs/architecture/FEEDBACK-LOOPS.md` — Template: FL-01 (CSS custom properties), FL-02 (mobile-first), FL-03 (placeholder), FL-04 (git push requires confirmation), FL-05 (protected files), FL-06–FL-09 (hard rules).

- `/docs/architecture/BREAKTHROUGHS.md` — Template format with B-01, B-02 placeholders and a generic worked example (form submit event listener bug).

- `/docs/architecture/ARCHITECTURE_EXTENSION.md` — CSS/JS conventions detail, design token reference tables (colours, spacing, typography), common pitfalls (5 entries), Schema.org reference, Logger system reference.

- `/docs/architecture/CODEBASE-AUDIT.md` — Exclusion lists, audit chunks (A1–A5), G1–G13 constraint check table, standard audit prompt, post-audit checklist.

- `/docs/architecture/REFLECTIVE-SYNC.md` — Session start/mid/end prompts, architecture sync prompts (Contradiction Hunt, Drift Check), Red Team Reset approach.

- `/docs/architecture/CLAUDE_ARCHITECTURE.md` — Document hierarchy (L1/L2/L3), reading order, entry prompts (Standard, Fresh, Deep), reference table.

- `/docs/architecture/CHECKPOINTS.md` — Rewritten generic version. Trigger table (9 triggers with what/where/format), mini-checkpoint format with example, Log Awareness section (git log commands, browser console patterns, triage flow), Implementation notes.

- `/docs/architecture/FE-VISUALISATION.md` — Rewritten generic version. Tool Decision Tree, Viewport Testing Matrix (5 breakpoints + phone landscape), Tool Reference (Python snap, Playwright, human screenshot, verbal), Wins FV-01 to FV-06, Hard Rules summary (7 rules), Quick-Reference table.

- `/docs/architecture/six-hats.md` — Genericized. Inversion examples replaced with: form validation failure, mobile device degradation, user abandoning key action. "This worked in practice" line generalized. Anchoring Prompt Templates rewritten for any web project (JS change, CSS change, performance change, HTML change, dependency addition). Regression check prompts generalized.

- `/docs/architecture/template-examples/GEMINI-CONSULTANCY.md` — "anthropicprinciple.ai" → "[PROJECT_NAME]" throughout. "iOS architect" → "senior web developer". 7 patterns (Architecture Audit, Stuck in Loop, Decision Validation, Research, Recursive Architecture Test, Delete Code/Quality Advocate, Model-Tiering). Payload Assembly Guide, trigger table.

- `/docs/architecture/template-examples/SERVERSIDE.md` — Full template skeleton: Overview table, Hosting table, Domain table, Environment Variables table, What Does Not Exist section, Security Posture table, CI/CD Pipeline table, Monitoring table. All values are [PLACEHOLDER].

- `/docs/architecture/template-examples/STANDARDS.md` — All status values reset to `⚠️ TBA`. Sections: Performance (including Animation if applicable), HTML, CSS (theme.css load order), JS, Accessibility (WCAG 2.1 AA), SEO. Open Issues table. Status Key.

- `/context/summaries/plan-rules.md` — 7 session operating rules + entry prompts.
- `/context/summaries/tasklist.md` — Task register template with status key.
- `/context/summaries/handoff_template.md` — Full handoff template with both checklists.
- `/templates/claude-templates.md` — 9 prompt templates (code review, commit, feature brief, bug investigation, handoff light/full, PageSpeed audit, accessibility audit, GH Actions debug, architecture sync).

**docs/plan/ (created in most recent incomplete session):**

- `/docs/plan/plan-rules.md` — Generic copy of anthropicprinciple's plan-rules.md (8 rules). Rule 1: always write handoff. Rule 2: all tasks in tasklist.md (task type table with FEAT/FIX/STYLE/HTML/DOCS/PERF/A11Y/SEO/DEPLOY/USER). Rule 3: mark OPEN not ASSUMED. Rule 4: archive old handoffs. Rule 5: context degradation recovery. Rule 6: checkpoint after every significant code change (mini-checkpoint format, auto-triggers listed). Rule 7: choose right session style (Hybrid/Fresh/Deep table). Entry prompts for all 3 styles. Reference table.

- `/docs/plan/tasklist.md` — Blank template: Feature Tasks, Style Tasks, HTML/Accessibility Tasks, Architecture Quality Gates (ARC1-ARC3), Testing/QA Tasks (T1-T3), Completed Tasks section, Status Key table.

- `/docs/plan/handoff_template.md` — Full template: Session Type, What Was Done (Goal/Outcome/Changes Made/Steps Covered), Key Facts & Decisions, Known Constraints, Open Questions (OPEN/ASSUMED format), State at Session End (Working/Not working), Checklist Status (both checklists), Next Session (priority order + entry prompt).

4. Errors and fixes:
- No technical errors encountered. All tool calls succeeded.
- The anthropicprinciple CLAUDE.md was modified by the user/linter during the session (system-reminder noted this) — noted but no action required as it was intentional.

5. Problem Solving:
- **Merging two ARCHITECTURE.md files**: The `docs/architecture/ARCHITECTURE.md` was the newer structured version (Core Structural Decisions, data flow diagrams) while `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` was the detailed technical version (CSS architecture detail, Clock Engine internals, Favicon Animator, Logger). Solution: used the structured version as the base and added the unique detailed sections from the technical version to create a comprehensive merged document.
- **Genericizing clock-specific content**: FE-VISUALISATION.md and CHECKPOINTS.md had deep clock-specific content (digit rendering, pattern descriptions, countdown state, rAF buffer specifics). Solution: rewrote both files completely, keeping the structural approach (tool decision tree, checkpoint triggers) but replacing all clock-specific details with generic web project equivalents.

Use this server to fetch current documentation whenever the user asks about a library, framework, SDK, API, CLI tool, or cloud service -- even well-known ones like React, Next.js, Prisma, Express, Tailwind, Django, or Spring Boot. # Session Handoff — 2026-04-06 — White-Label Starter Scaffold

> **This is an example handoff** showing what a completed setup session looks like.
> Copy `handoff_template.md` to write your own. This file is for reference only.

## Session Type
Docs / Architecture — initial white-label scaffold for AI-assisted web development

---

## What Was Done

### Goal
Build a complete, reusable white-label project starter that any developer can clone and use as the foundation for a new Claude Code–assisted web project.

### Outcome
Resolved — all architecture docs, plan system templates, CLAUDE.md, and README.md created. Project is ready to clone and adapt.

### Changes Made

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` | Created — generic architecture template with [PROJECT_NAME] placeholders throughout |
| `docs/SYSTEM.md` | Created — developer rules: CSS/JS/file naming, file organisation map, commit conventions |
| `docs/architecture/CORE_PATTERNS.md` | Created — G1–G13 constraint table, code patterns with correct/wrong examples, quick regression checklist |
| `docs/architecture/DECISIONS.md` | Created — ADR template; ADR-001 (placeholder), ADR-002 (CSS custom properties), ADR-003 (mobile-first) |
| `docs/architecture/FEEDBACK-LOOPS.md` | Created — FL-01 to FL-09 feedback loop records template |
| `docs/architecture/BREAKTHROUGHS.md` | Created — B-01/B-02 placeholders + worked generic debugging example |
| `docs/architecture/ARCHITECTURE_EXTENSION.md` | Created — CSS/JS conventions, design token reference, common pitfalls, Schema.org reference |
| `docs/architecture/CODEBASE-AUDIT.md` | Created — audit chunks A1–A5, G1–G13 check table, standard audit prompt, post-audit checklist |
| `docs/architecture/REFLECTIVE-SYNC.md` | Created — session start/mid/end prompts, architecture sync (Contradiction Hunt, Drift Check) |
| `docs/architecture/CLAUDE_ARCHITECTURE.md` | Created — document hierarchy L1/L2/L3, reading order, 3 session entry prompt styles |
| `docs/architecture/CHECKPOINTS.md` | Created — 9 auto-trigger table, mini-checkpoint format, Log Awareness section |
| `docs/architecture/FE-VISUALISATION.md` | Created — tool decision tree, viewport testing matrix (5 breakpoints + landscape), 6 wins |
| `docs/architecture/six-hats.md` | Created — Six Hats model, Inversion, Anchoring Prompts, Regression Checks — all generic |
| `docs/architecture/template-examples/GEMINI-CONSULTANCY.md` | Created — 7 Gemini consultation patterns, payload assembly guide, trigger table |
| `docs/architecture/template-examples/SERVERSIDE.md` | Created — server-side architecture template; all values [PLACEHOLDER] |
| `docs/architecture/template-examples/STANDARDS.md` | Created — quality standards tables (Performance, HTML, CSS, JS, A11Y, SEO); all ⚠️ TBA |
| `CLAUDE.md` | Created — white-label Claude Code instructions skeleton; TBA markers where project-specific |
| `README.md` | Created — public-facing project readme with repo structure diagram |
| `docs/plan/plan-rules.md` | Created — 8-rule session management system (copy of anthropicprinciple's, genericised) |
| `docs/plan/tasklist.md` | Created — blank task register template |
| `docs/plan/handoff_template.md` | Created — full handoff template with both checklists |
| `docs/plan/example-tasklist.md` | Created — this session's tasks in example tasklist format |
| `docs/plan/example-handoff_2026-04-06.md` | Created — this file |

### Steps Covered

1. Merged `docs/architecture/ARCHITECTURE.md` into `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` for a parent project — used as reference for white-label version
2. Created `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` and `docs/SYSTEM.md` — top-level generic architecture templates
3. Created all 11 files in `docs/architecture/` — detailed architecture, patterns, decisions, feedback loops, breakthroughs, audit guide, reflective sync, checkpoints, FE visualisation, six hats thinking, Claude architecture
4. Created `docs/architecture/template-examples/` — GEMINI-CONSULTANCY.md, SERVERSIDE.md, STANDARDS.md
5. Created `CLAUDE.md` — white-label Claude Code session instructions skeleton
6. Created `README.md` — public-facing project readme
7. Created `docs/plan/` system — plan-rules.md, tasklist.md, handoff_template.md, example files

---

## Key Facts & Decisions

| Item | Value / Decision |
|------|-----------------|
| All docs genericised | No project-specific references; all anthropicprinciple content replaced with [PROJECT_NAME] or TBA |
| CSS load order | `theme.css` → `global.css` → `grid.css` → `components.css` → `custom.css` → `utilities.css` |
| G1–G13 constraints | Defined in CORE_PATTERNS.md — these are the do-not-break rules for any session |
| Session styles | Hybrid (default) / Fresh (Red Team) / Deep (Bloated) — see plan-rules.md Rule 7 |
| Checkpoint trigger | After every Write or Edit to a source file — see plan-rules.md Rule 6 |
| Gemini second model | Used for architecture audits, contradiction hunts, stuck-in-loop recovery — see GEMINI-CONSULTANCY.md |
| Git push confirmation | Always requires explicit user confirmation — never autonomous (FL-04) |

---

## Known Constraints

- `docs/plan/archive/` directory exists but is empty — no handoffs to archive at project start
- All `[PROJECT_NAME]` placeholders in ARCHITECTURE.md, SYSTEM.md, CLAUDE.md must be replaced on project setup
- CSS token names in ARCHITECTURE_EXTENSION.md (`--color-bg`, `--space-md`, etc.) are suggestions — adapt to project brand
- `template-examples/` files are reference-only — not included in active session reads unless specifically needed
- `docs/init/` folder (setup instructions) can be deleted once the project is configured

---

## Open Questions

```
OPEN: Should docs/init/ be removed from the template repo or kept for reference?
ASSUMED: Keep for now — acts as setup guide for first-time users. Remove once project is configured.

OPEN: Should a starter index.html and styles/ skeleton be included in the template?
ASSUMED: No — this is a documentation scaffold only. Code files are project-specific.
```

---

## State at Session End

**Working:**
- All 23 documentation files created and committed
- `docs/plan/` system fully operational — rules, templates, and examples in place
- CLAUDE.md ready to adapt for any new project
- README.md describes the project clearly

**Not working / deferred:**
- No source code exists yet — `index.html`, `styles/`, `js/` are empty or absent
- STANDARDS.md all entries are `⚠️ TBA` — no code to validate against yet
- ADR-001 in DECISIONS.md is a placeholder — fill when first major technical decision is made
- FE-VISUALISATION.md wins (FV-01 to FV-06) are placeholders — fill as the project accumulates visual debugging history

---

## Checklist Status

### Your Checklist
- [x] All setup files created and committed
- [ ] Responsive at 320 / 480 / 768 / 1024 / 1440px — N/A (no code yet)
- [ ] Landscape mode fits viewport — N/A
- [ ] Dark mode verified at OS level — N/A
- [ ] PageSpeed ≥ 95 — N/A

### Claude's Checklist
- [x] No inline styles — N/A (no HTML yet)
- [x] No `!important` — N/A (no CSS yet)
- [x] No `max-width` media queries — N/A
- [x] CSS custom properties for all colours/spacing — documented in ARCHITECTURE_EXTENSION.md
- [x] `defer` on all scripts — documented in CORE_PATTERNS.md G9
- [x] Schema.org JSON-LD present — documented in ARCHITECTURE.md §SEO
- [x] Open Graph tags present — documented in ARCHITECTURE.md §SEO

---

## Next Session

Priority order:

1. **Configure the project** — replace all `[PROJECT_NAME]` placeholders in CLAUDE.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, SYSTEM.md with the actual project name and details
2. **Populate design tokens** — fill brand colours, spacing scale, typography into ARCHITECTURE.md §Colour Palette and ARCHITECTURE_EXTENSION.md design token tables
3. **Build first HTML page** — create `index.html` with correct doctype, meta, semantic structure, and CSS load order per SYSTEM.md

**Entry prompt:**
```
Read docs/plan/example-handoff_2026-04-06.md, docs/plan/tasklist.md, and docs/ARCHITECTURE.md.
Task: Configure the project — replace [PROJECT_NAME] placeholders and populate design tokens.

## Key Facts & Decisions

| Item | Value / Decision |

| All docs genericised | No project-specific references; all anthropicprinciple content replaced with [PROJECT_NAME] or TBA |
| CSS load order | `theme.css` → `global.css` → `grid.css` → `components.css` → `custom.css` → `utilities.css` |

| Checkpoint trigger | After every Write or Edit to a source file — see plan-rules.md Rule 6 |
| Gemini second model | Used for architecture audits, contradiction hunts, stuck-in-loop recovery — see GEMINI-CONSULTANCY.md |
| Git push confirmation | Always requires explicit user confirmation — never autonomous (FL-04) |
